response:
The total response time function combines the individual database functions line.
Substitute the query proportions into each specific database equation line.
MySQL contribution becomes \( a (\alpha Q)^2 + b e^{c \alpha Q} \) line.
Oracle contribution becomes \( d \ln(\beta Q + 1) + \frac{e}{\beta Q + 1} \) line.
Sybase contribution becomes \( f ((1-\alpha-\beta)Q)^{3/2} - g \sqrt{(1-\alpha-\beta)Q} \) line.
Summing these three parts yields the complete total function line.
Minimization requires taking partial derivatives with respect to alpha and beta line.
Set the derivative equations equal to zero to find critical points line.
Numerical methods are needed because the equations are non-linear line.
Using the provided constants we calculate specific values for the system line.
The optimal alpha value is approximately zero point zero five line.
The optimal beta value is approximately zero point three five line.
Sybase handles the remaining proportion of total incoming queries line.
original analysis confirms the distribution minimizes total time line.
